title: STANDARD FOR EDIBLE CASEIN PRODUCTS CXS 290-1995

sections.section: STANDARD FOR EDIBLE CASEIN PRODUCTS CXS 290-1995
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Edible Casein Products (CXS 290-1995).
Provides the adoption and amendment history: adopted 1995, revised 2001, and amended in 2010, 2013, 2014, 2016, 2018, 2022 and 2023.
Notes that following the Codex Commission decisions in December 2022, amendments were made to Section 7.2 on labelling of non‑retail containers.

States that after December 2023 decisions, the standard's food additive provisions were amended and incorporated into the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, CXS 192-1995) to align additive provisions.
Scope: applies to edible acid casein, edible rennet casein and edible caseinate intended for direct consumption or further processing, as described in Section 2 of the standard.

Defines three milk products: edible acid casein (coagulum from acid-precipitated skimmed milk or milk-derived products, separated, washed and dried), edible rennet casein (coagulum from rennet or coagulating enzymes from skimmed milk or milk-derived products, separated, washed and dried) and edible caseinate (product obtained by treating edible casein or casein curd with neutralizing agents followed by drying).
Section heading only for essential composition and quality factors; no substantive provisions in this item.
Specifies raw materials as skimmed milk and/or other products obtained from milk.
Permits starter cultures of harmless lactic acid bacteria, rennet or other safe coagulating enzymes, and potable water as ingredients.

Specifies compositional limits for rennet casein, acid casein and caseinates (e.g., minimum milk protein in dry matter: rennet 84.0%, acid 90.0%, caseinates 88.0%; minimum casein in milk protein 95.0% for all; maximum water rennet/acid 12.0%, caseinates 8.0%; maximum milkfat 2.0%; ash, lactose, free acid and pH limits as listed) and states that modifications allowed under Section 4.3.3 of the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms may be made but any changes beyond the specified minima/maxima for listed components are non-compliant.

Only additive functional classes indicated as technologically justified may be used; acceptable classes for edible casein products include acidity regulators, anticaking agents, bulking agents and emulsifiers, used in accordance with the referenced General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) and applicable tables.

Requires products and the milk used in their manufacture to comply with maximum levels for contaminants and toxins in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides in milk.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and requires compliance with any applicable microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Labelling section heading noting that, in addition to general Codex labelling standards, specific provisions for these products follow in subsequent clauses.
Prescribes permitted names: 'Edible acid casein', 'Edible caseinate' and 'Edible rennet casein', and requires edible caseinate to be accompanied on the label by an indication of the cation used.

States labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021) and be consistent with the product descriptions in Section 2 and compositions in Section 3.3.
Specifies that methods of analysis and sampling for compliance checking shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard's provisions.
Appendix heading stating the additional information is non-binding and does not affect the essential preceding provisions on product identity, naming and safety.
Section heading only for other quality factors; no substantive content in this item.
Physical appearance requirement: product should be white to pale cream and free from lumps that do not break up under slight pressure.
Flavour and odour requirement: not more than slight foreign flavours and odours and the product must be free from offensive flavours and odours.
Section heading only for processing aids; substantive details are provided in ensuing subclauses.

Lists acids permitted for precipitation (with INS numbers): acetic acid (260), lactic acid (270, L-, D- and DL-), citric acid (330), orthophosphoric acid (338), hydrochloric acid (507) and sulphuric acid (513).
Permits calcium chloride (INS 509) for renneting enhancement purposes.
Specifies maximum sediment (scorched particles) per 25 g: rennet casein 15 mg, acid casein 22.5 mg, and caseinates 22.5 mg for spray-dried and 81.5 mg for roller-dried.

Specifies maximum sediment (scorched particles) limits for casein products: rennet casein 15 mg/25 g, acid casein 22.5 mg/25 g, and caseinates 22.5 mg/25 g (spray‑dried) or 81.5 mg/25 g (roller‑dried).
For rennet casein, sets maximum sediment (scorched particles) at 15 mg per 25 g (table also lists limits for acid casein and caseinates).
For acid casein, sets maximum sediment (scorched particles) at 22.5 mg per 25 g (table also lists limits for rennet casein and caseinates).
For caseinates, sets maximum sediment (scorched particles) limits of 22.5 mg/25 g for spray‑dried and 81.5 mg/25 g for roller‑dried; table also shows rennet and acid casein limits.
States that compliance testing must use methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard.
